title: AIDS/HIV Prevention Counseling - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > AIDS/HIV Prevention Counseling 19 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT TESTING4ALL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rapid HIV testing, syphilis, hepatitis C, and prevention counseling. 520 NW Davis Street Suite 215 Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-4pm Email (503) 223-5907 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management to individuals living with HIV and AIDS. This program provides expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care appointments, transitional housing and rent payment assistance, disease management education, referrals to resources for basic needs, medical and dental services, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 342-5088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT PRISM HEALTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides primary health care and behavioral health. Offers laboratory and pharmacy services. 2236 SE Belmont Street Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-5pm, Thursday 9am-5pm Email (503) 445-7699 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTERS FOR DISEASE CONTROL AND PREVENTION GET PREP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a locator tool to find healthcare providers who prescribe PrEP, a medication that helps prevent HIV infection. 1600 Clifton Road Atlanta, GA 30329 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details GRANT COUNTY Email (541) 342-5088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIV ALLIANCE HIV ALLIANCE MARION COUNTY HIV AND HEPATITIS C TESTING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential HIV and hepatitis C (HCV) testing. 3855 Wolverine Street NE Suite 6 Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Individuals considered to be high-risk for contracting HIV or HCV Hours: Monday/Wednesday 3pm-7pm Email (541) 342-5088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH SYRINGE EXCHANGE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services include used points exchange for new points, wound care supplies, safer sex supplies, risk reduction counseling, and referrals to medical care services, such as sexually transmitted disease screening, HIV testing, mental health services, alcohol and drug treatment, medication assisted treatment, and naloxone. 140 S Holly Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Intravenous drug users age 18 and older, none for HIV testing Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 2pm-4pm HIV testing by appointment: Monday-Friday 9-11am, 1pm-4pm Email (541) 774-8209 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH ALBANY HIV/AIDS RAPID TESTING AND PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential rapid testing for HIV/AIDS, syphilis, and hepatitis C. Offers prevention education and treatment support. Assists with access to HIV medications, transportation to medical appointments, transitional housing, disease management education, and referrals for basic needs, employment, and substance use treatment. 1050 SW 7th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 967-3888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT HIV COMMUNITY TEST SITE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides anonymous and confidential HIV, hepatitis C, and syphilis testing. HIV testing is done by taking a blood or oral sample, with rapid 20-minute test results available for those who qualify. Results are available by phone or in person. Condoms and other safe sex supplies also available. Offers HIV prevention counseling and PrEP (PRe Exposure Prophylaxis) navigation. 619 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: -Standard HIV test: Unrestricted -Rapid HIV test: Men who have or have had sex with men, injection drug users, and partners of people living with HIV -Hepatitis C test: Any individual with a history of injection drug use -Syphilis test: Men who have sex with men, trans folks who may be at risk Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4:30pm Test Results Line: Monday-Friday 1:30pm-4pm Email (503) 988-3700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST NARA NW INDIAN HEALTH CLINIC REPRODUCTIVE AND SEXUAL HEALTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a wide range of general clinic health services, including physical exams, treatment of minor injuries, immunizations, well baby/child clinics, nutrition counseling, mental health evaluations and counseling services, diabetes testing, sexually transmitted infections testing, education and treatment, breast cancer screening, tobacco cessation programs, addictions assessment and treatment, and Oregon Health Plan (OHP) eligibility assistance. 703 NE Hancock Street Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as Native American or Alaskan Native Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-7pm Email (503) 230-9875 Get Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-7pm Email (971) 279-4800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN INJECTION DRUG USERS HEALTH SERVICES NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a syringe exchange to prevent the spread of HIV and other diseases among drug injectors. In addition to safer injection supplies, participants can receive counseling, safer sex information and supplies, free and anonymous HIV testing and Hepatitis C testing, as well as other drug treatment referrals. Outside In's syringe exchange policy is a one for one (1:1) program, meaning users get an even exchange for the amount of syringes they bring in, however no one will be turned away for not having returns. 1219 SW Main Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday noon-5pm Email (503) 535-3826 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN INJECTION DRUG USERS HEALTH SERVICES NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a syringe exchange to prevent the spread of HIV and other diseases among drug injectors. In addition to safer injection supplies, participants can receive counseling, safer sex information and supplies, free and anonymous HIV testing and Hepatitis C testing, as well as other drug treatment referrals. Outside In's syringe exchange policy is a one for one (1:1) program, meaning users get an even exchange for the amount of syringes they bring in, however no one will be turned away for not having returns. 8800 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday noon-5pm Email (503) 535-3826 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN INJECTION DRUG USERS HEALTH SERVICES NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a syringe exchange to prevent the spread of HIV and other diseases among drug injectors.
